data.title: ZOOZ Power soars after shareholders approve financing to back Bitcoin strategy
data.description:
• ZOOZ Power (NASDAQ:ZOOZ) shareholders have approved all proposals related to the company’s previously announced $180 million private placement (PIPE) and Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy.
  • Key proposals approved include issuance of ordinary shares and warrants in connection with the offering, including $5 million sold in the initial private placement; amendment to the company’s Articles of Association to increase the authorized share capital; election of Alberto Franco and Jonas Grossman to the company’s board of directors upon the closing date. ZOOZ expects to close the PIPE during the week of Sept. 22, 2025.
  • Proceeds from the offering will primarily be used to launch ZOOZ’s Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy. The company intends to allocate around 95% of the net proceeds to purchase and hold Bitcoin on its balance sheet.
  • ZOOZ +199.5% premarket to $6.98
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BlackRock favors gold, bitcoin, growth, and international stocks after Fed rate cut
data.description:
The Federal Reserve’s decision earlier this week to cut interest rates by 25 basis points marked the first reduction since December 2024, signaling a renewed phase of monetary easing. Investors and market participants are now assessing the implications of the move, with BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager, offering a roadmap for where opportunities may emerge.

According to BlackRock, the rate cut strengthens the investment case for large-cap growth stocks, international equities, and alternative assets such as gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and bitcoin (BTC-USD). The firm highlighted that lower borrowing costs could support corporate earnings and valuations, particularly for growth-oriented companies. 

“We believe the U.S. economy will avoid recession for the foreseeable future; if that proves true, large-cap growth stocks are most likely to benefit from a lower rate environment,” BlackRock stated in a recent investor note. 

At the same time, the Federal Open Market Committee’s policy shift is expected to exert downward pressure on the U.S. dollar (DXY). A weaker currency “may further boost the appeal of international equities as well as portfolio diversifiers such as gold and bitcoin,” the investment institution added. 

With monetary policy entering a more accommodative phase, BlackRock suggests that investors remain flexible, as diversification may become increasingly valuable in the months ahead. 

Outlined below are a group of ETFs that fit the bill towards BlackRock’s viewpoint. 

Large-Cap Growth ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VUG), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NYSEARCA:IVW), (NYSEARCA:SCHG), (NYSEARCA:MGK), (NASDAQ:IUSG), (NYSEARCA:VOOG), and (NYSEARCA:JGRO).

International ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VEA), (BATS:IEFA), (NYSEARCA:EFA), (NYSEARCA:SCHF), (NYSEARCA:SPDW), (NYSEARCA:IDEV), (NYSEARCA:FNDF), and (NYSEARCA:VSS).

Gold ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:GLDM), (NYSEARCA:SGOL), (NYSEARCA:IAUM), (NYSEARCA:OUNZ), (BATS:AAAU), (NYSEARCA:BAR), VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDX), VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDXJ), iShares MSCI Global Gold Miners ETF (NASDAQ:RING), and Direxion Daily Gold Miners Index Bull 2x Shares (NYSEARCA:NUGT).

Bitcoin ETFs: (NASDAQ:IBIT), (BATS:FBTC), (NYSEARCA:GBTC), (NYSEARCA:BTC), (BATS:ARKB), (NYSEARCA:BITO), and (BATS:HODL).

data.title: Indicators show consumer appetite for Apple's iPhone 17 stronger than expected; JP Morgan raises targets
data.description:
Indicators demonstrate consumers have a stronger-than-expected appetite for Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) new iPhone 17, which became generally available today.

Shares edged up 1% during early Friday trading.

These indicators prompted J.P. Morgan to increase its price target to $280 from $255 and maintain its Overweight rating. The firm also raised its revenue and earnings per share estimates.

The "early demand indications are outlining the opportunity for the volume cycle to not only track higher than our initial expectations (which incorporated concerns around digestion following a pull-forward in iPhone 16 series), but also upside to iPhone 16 series volumes on a y/y basis," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Samik Chatterjee, in a Friday investor note. "Led by the improved volume outlook, we are now forecasting iPhone volumes of 236 mn in FY26, which will represent a modest +2% y/y increase, which when combined with mix tailwinds will, in our view, support mid- to high-single digit iPhone revenue growth."

J.P. Morgan increased full fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS to $8.20 from $7.55 and full fiscal 2027 to $9.50 from $8.45. The investment firm increased its iPhone revenue estimate for fiscal 2026 to $223B from $200B and fiscal 2027 to $250B from $212B.

"As we look beyond FY26, consumer and investor excitement will shift to the foldable iPhone, which will feature another major form factor redesign/launch," Chatterjee said. "In addition to the product roadmap, FY27 will bring changes in relation to the product launch cadence with high-end iPhone 18 series launched in the fall heading into FY27, followed by the launch of base iPhone 18 series models in middle of FY27."

On Wednesday, Morgan Stanley also noted that "lead time analysis suggests that early demand for the iPhone 17 models is stronger than at the onset of the iPhone 16 cycle last year, a positive early indication that iPhone replacement cycles could be stabilizing and upgrade rates could be improving."

Companies that provide vital components for the iPhone 17 include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), Qualcomm (QCOM) and Corning (GLW).

data.title: Homebuilder stocks edge down as Lennar posts double-miss on Q3 results
data.description:
A slew of homebuilder stocks slipped in Friday premarket trading as Lennar (NYSE:LEN), one of the U.S.'s largest builders, turned in worse-than-expected fiscal Q3 numbers that reflect incentives and price cuts to meet soft market conditions.

Overall, even with stock index futures ticking up, the iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF (BATS:ITB) edged down 0.6% at 9:05 a.m. ET. The SPDR S&P Homebuilders ETF (NYSEARCA:XHB) dipped 0.5% and the Direxion Daily Homebuilders & Supplies Bull 3X Shares ETF (NYSEARCA:NAIL) slid 2.4%.

By name, D.R. Horton (DHI) retreated 1.5%, KB Home (KBH) fell 1.6%, PulteGroup (PHM) -0.8%, Toll Brothers (TOL) -0.9%, Tri Pointe Homes (TPH) -0.7%, Taylor Morrison Home (TMHC) -0.4%. 

However, several builders eked out gains, including Meritage Homes (MTH), advancing 1.6%, and Beazer Homes (BZH) +0.1%.

In addition to underwhelming FQ3 earnings and revenue, Lennar (NYSE:LEN), which saw its stock slide 3.8% at press time, delivered FQ4 new orders guidance that misses the consensus at the midpoint.

It expects new orders of 20K-21K (midpoint 20.5K) for the quarter, compared with the 20K Visible Alpha estimate.

During the quarter ended Aug. 31, 2025, Lennar (LEN) "used targeted incentives, including mortgage rate buydowns, to sustain momentum" in starts and sales, Jon Jaffe, the company's co-CEO and president, had said in a statement.

As it relates to broader macro risks, "interest rates remained elevated throughout the third quarter, but then declined towards the quarter's end," Jaffe added. "This downward trend, paired with the Fed's recent rate cut, gives us optimism as we head into the fourth quarter. Therefore, we believe that now is a good time to moderate our volume and allow the market to catch up."

data.title: Opendoor appoints interim CFO following Selim Freiha’s departure
data.description:
• Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN) appointed Christy Schwartz as interim chief financial officer, effective September 30, 2025, replacing Selim Freiha, who stepped down on September 19, 2025.
  • Schwartz previously served as Opendoor’s interim CFO (Dec 2022–Nov 2024), chief accounting officer (Mar 2021–May 2025).
  • The interim CFO role will end on the earlier of May 15, 2026, or 30 days after a new CFO is appointed.
  • Schwartz will receive a $1.2M annual base salary and a grant of 400,641 RSUs, vesting in three equal tranches through May 2026. 
  • OPEN shares up 2.2% premarket on Friday. 
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: 3 out of 5 companies miss EPS estimates this week: Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed at record highs on Thursday, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points. Stocks were propelled by gains in big tech, and cybersecurity.

Markets were helped by strong premarket action from tech firms Intel (INTC) and Nvidia (NVDA) after the firms announced a blockbuster deal to co-develop PC and data center chips. Simultaneously, Nvidia said it would take a $5B stake in Intel.

Also lifting sentiment, the FOMC on Wednesday announced its decision, trimming its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.00%-4.25%. 

“It took the market half a day, but it realized that cutting rates in an environment of solid GDP growth and rising inflation is fuel for cyclical sectors,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen said.

Earnings Roundup: 

Out of the five companies that reported earnings this week, only two of them beat EPS estimates, while four of them surpassed revenue expectations. 

Let’s take a look at all the companies that reported earnings this week. 

FedEx Corporation (FDX) topped expectations with its FQ1 earnings report. The Memphis-based shipping giant reported revenue was up 2.8% year-over-year to $22.2 billion in FQ1. EPS came in at $3.83 vs. $3.61 consensus and $3.60 a year ago.

The company said Federal Express segment operating results improved during the quarter, driven by higher U.S. domestic and international priority package yields, continued cost savings from transformation initiatives, and increased U.S. domestic package volume. 

Darden Restaurants (DRI) reported sales increased by 10.4% in FQ1 to $3.0 billion, driven by a blended same-restaurant sales increase and sales from the acquisition of 103 Chuy's Tex Mex restaurants and 22 net new restaurants.

In terms of guidance, the company sees FY26 revenue growth of +7.5% to +8.5%. Full-year comparable sales growth of +2.5% to +3.5% is anticipated. The midpoint of the range is slightly below the consensus expectation of +3.1%.

Lennar Corporation (LEN) reported third quarter adjusted earnings per share of $2.00, missing estimates by $0.09, on revenue of $8.81B, a decline of more than 6% from last year. 

General Mills (GIS) posted a mixed FQ1 earnings report. The company topped profit estimates but came up short of expectations with its revenue and organic sales marks.

Sales decreased 7.7% during FQ4 to $4.5 billion for the quarter that ended on August 24, driven lower by lower pound volume and unfavorable net price realization and mix. 

FactSet Research Systems (FDS) turned in fiscal Q4 earnings that missed the average analyst estimate, in addition to soft revenue guidance for fiscal 2026.

The financial digital platform said it expects FY26 GAAP revenue of $2,423M-$2,448M vs. $2.45B consensus. Meanwhile, Q4 2025 adjusted EPS of $4.05, fell from $4.27 in Q3 but gained 8.3% from a year earlier.

Earnings next week:  

Several companies are scheduled to report earnings next week, including Firefly Aerospace (FLY) and Genfit (GNFT) on Monday, with Micron (MU), AutoZone (AZO), AAR (AIR), and Apogee (APOG) on Tuesday. Meanwhile, Wednesday will see earnings reports from Cintas (CTAS), Worthington Steel (WS), Stitch Fix (SFIX) and Comtech (CMTL). 

Names such as Costco (COST), Accenture (ACN), CarMax (KMX), BlackBerry (BB), and Inventiva (IVA) will report earnings on Thursday, with Deep Yellow (OTCQX:DYLLF), Taylor Devices (TAYD), and Globus Maritime (GLBS) reporting on Friday.

data.title: Platinum Analytics Cayman prices $8M IPO, to begin trading on Nasdaq
data.description:
• Platinum Analytics Cayman (PLTS) priced its initial public offering at $4/share, offering 2M Class A ordinary shares for $8M in gross proceeds.
  • The shares are approved for listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market and will begin trading on September 19, 2025, under the ticker “PLTS.”
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to 300,000 additional shares to cover over-allotments.
  • The IPO is expected to close on September 22, 2025.
  • Net proceeds will be allocated as follows: ~$2.7M (40%) for R&D, ~$2.1M (30%) for marketing and sales expansion, and ~$2.0M (30%) for IT and license procurement.
  • PLTS provides FX trading software, data analytics, and tech solutions, with a focus on Asia and emerging markets.
  • Source: Press release
